title: Intel 18A wafer-to-wafer yield issues fixed, report claims says production up to 15,000 wafers per month at both sites
summary: Intel reportedly resolves wafer-to-wafer yield issues in its 18A process technology, potentially boosting production to 15,000 wafers per month at both manufacturing sites. This achievement addresses one of the significant challenges facing the technology. Future success may depend on resolving remaining issues.
insight: Fixing one yield issue is a crucial step forward for Intel 18A technology

title: GitHub thumbs nose at Sony's controversial end to physical media with its introduction of Repo CDs offers limited run of 1,000 CD-ROM copies of public GitHub repos for preservation
summary: GitHub offers physical CD-ROM copies of public repos for preservation, countering Sony's digital-only shift.
insight: Preserving digital legacy through physical media for long-term archive purposes.

title: This 144-in-1 electric screwdriver set is a must-buy toolkit for PC building and hobbyists, now under $40 20% saving on kit with a second precision driver, 120 magnetic bits, and 22 maintenance tools for builds and repairs
summary: Get a comprehensive 144-in-1 repair toolkit for PC building and hobbyists at a 20% discount, now under $40. This set includes 120 magnetic bits, a second precision driver, and 22 maintenance tools for various builds and repairs.
insight: Comprehensive toolkit for DIY enthusiasts at an unbeatable price point

title: Scientists have created a 3D-printed remote-controlled cyborg cockroach equipped with IR cameras living insects fitted with flexible 'diving suit' can survive and move underwater for three hours
summary: Scientists in Singapore have developed remote-controlled cyborg cockroaches with IR cameras and scuba suits, enabling them to survive and move underwater for three hours. These cockroaches can be used in rescue operations and extreme environment exploration. This technology has potential applications in search and rescue missions.
insight: Robust, low-cost, and flexible robots for extreme environment exploration.

title: Jensen Huang s iconic signed leather jacket expected to fetch up to $60,000 in charity auction Sotheby s says item was worn at a Foxconn Tech Day in 2023 and the signature has been professionally authenticated
summary: Jensen Huang's signed leather jacket is up for auction, estimated at $40,000 to $60,000, with proceeds going to charity. The jacket was worn at a Foxconn Tech Day in 2023 and the signature has been professionally authenticated. The auction is being held by Sotheby's.
insight: Rare tech memorabilia supports charity with significant potential value realized

title: SK hynix to invest $712.5 billion in South Korean operations Cheongju NAND expansion, Yongin Semiconductor Cluster for DRAM detailed
summary: SK hynix plans $712.5 billion investment in South Korea, mainly in NAND and packaging facilities.
insight: Massive investment in memory production fuels South Korea's tech industry growth

title: Palantir CEO Alex Karp claims AI companies are stealing customers' data while charging them for unproductive tokens says 'livid' businesses 'are paying for tokens that create no value'
summary: Palantir CEO Alex Karp accuses AI companies of stealing customer data, charging for unproductive services.
insight: Data exploitation undermines trust in AI industry's business models.

title: Nvidia offers to take a cut of AI cloud revenue on top of hardware sales in new optional financing vehicle trades tokens for revenue cut
summary: Nvidia is offering a new business model where it takes a cut of AI cloud revenue on top of hardware sales through an optional financing vehicle that trades tokens for a revenue share.
insight: Nvidia doubles its revenue stream with AI cloud deal.

title: U.S PC shipments drop 7%, market isn't expected to bounce back until 2029 price hikes and component shortages take hold as PC market declines, Omdia report suggests
summary: U.S PC shipments drop 7% in 2026, expected to worsen, then rebound by 2029.
insight: Price hikes and shortages may boost PC demand in future years.

title: Supermicro denies that its offices were raided by Taiwanese authorities in Nvidia GPU smuggling case company says that it coordinated with the police and provided access to investigated employees workstations and gadgets
summary: Supermicro denies raid allegations, claims cooperation with Taiwanese authorities and police.
insight: Corporate reputation at stake in high-profile smuggling case investigation.

title: Digital archivists rush to save PS3 game data before Sony shuts down the store forever in 2027 RPCS3 emulator urges users to preserve all content
summary: Sony's shutdown of PSN for PS3 consoles in 2027 prompts digital archivists to act, urging users to preserve game data. This requires immediate action to save game content before it's lost forever. Preservationists must now wake up to save the data.
insight: Preserving PS3 game data is crucial before Sony's permanent shutdown.

title: Montech NX600 Review: A budget dual tower with jet-engine fans
summary: The Montech NX600 is a budget-friendly dual-tower air cooler featuring six heatpipes and two thick 120mm fans. It's designed to provide efficient cooling for high-performance processors. Our review benchmarks its thermal efficiency on AMD's Ryzen 9 9950X3D.
insight: Affordable high-performance cooling for demanding CPU workloads.

title: Meta reportedly plans to rent out its AI compute, sending AI stocks tumbling 'Meta Compute' would put company in direct competition with AWS
summary: Meta is reportedly planning to rent out its AI compute capacity, competing with AWS. This move could give developers access to Meta's AI infrastructure and computing power. The service model is still in development.
insight: Meta's entry into AI computing disrupts existing cloud service market

title: Asus B850-Creator Wifi Neo motherboard review: AM5 Creator mobo looks the part, but is missing useful features
summary: Asus' B850-Creator Wifi Neo boasts impressive features but lacks USB ports and is pricey.
insight: Creator motherboard prioritizes performance over practical features and connectivity options.

title: Intel hikes pricing for its flagship desktop PC chips by up to $50 official Core Ultra 270K Plus and 250K Plus product pages now recommend prices of up to $349 and $229, respectively
summary: Intel has increased the prices of its flagship desktop PC chips, with prices now ranging from $229 to $349. The Arrow Lake Refresh processors, which debut in March, have seen a noticeable price hike. This change may impact consumer purchasing decisions and market competition.
insight: Price increases affect consumer affordability and market dynamics.

title: OpenAI mulling giving US gov't a 5% stake in the company, days after Washington delayed GPT-5.6 Altman reportedly wants every leading U.S. AI lab paying into an Alaska-style public fund
summary: OpenAI considers a 5% stake for the US government to gain regulatory clarity. Sam Altman proposed an Alaska-style public fund for AI labs. This was discussed with senior US officials.
insight: Government investment could ensure AI regulation and public safety accountability.

title: Startup activates nuclear microreactor live on stage to power an Nvidia RTX Spark desktop PC firm working with Nvidia to build a 30MW closed loop AI factory that doesn t use local water
summary: Valar Atomics demonstrated its microreactor powering an Nvidia RTX Spark unit. The startup also announced a partnership with Nvidia to build a 30MW AI data center that uses a closed-loop system, eliminating the need for local water. This move could revolutionize the field of nuclear energy and AI computing.
insight: Game-changing nuclear power meets sustainable AI computing infrastructure

title: HALO TOUCH V2 A customizable desktop USB hub with rotary encoder fitted with round touchscreen display
summary: The HALO TOUCH V2 is a customizable desktop USB hub featuring a round touchscreen and rotary encoder, offering system monitoring, media playback, and productivity features.
insight: Seamless desktop integration for workstations, home offices, and personal productivity.

title: ESP32-C5 dual-band WiFi 6 and Bluetooth LE IoT board features 1.47-inch color LCD
summary: The Waveshare ESP32-C5-LCD-1.47 features a dual-band WiFi 6 ESP32-C5 chip with a 1.47-inch 262K-color display and various expansion options for IoT projects.
insight: Dual-band WiFi 6 connectivity for enhanced IoT performance and range.

title: NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano/NX-based Mekotronics AI Box targets humanoid robots, Smart Cities management, transportation applications
summary: Mekotronics' AI Box is an NVIDIA Jetson Orin-based embedded computer designed for various applications, including humanoid robots, smart transportation, and Smart Cities management. It offers standard interfaces and up to 157 TOPS processing power. The AI Box is suitable for multiple industries and use cases.
insight: AI-powered edge computing for diverse industries and applications emerges.

title: Titan Mini is a smaller Renesas RA8P1-based Edge AI board for RT-Thread RTOS development
summary: The Titan Mini is a compact, cost-effective edge AI board for AIoT applications and RT-Thread RTOS development, featuring a Renesas RA8P1 microcontroller with a lower-capacity memory and storage configuration compared to its predecessor.
insight: Smaller, affordable edge AI board for AIoT and RTOS development.

title: Qualcomm unveils robot reference design, IQ10
summary: Qualcomm unveiled the IQ10 robot reference design during Computex in Taiwan, led by CEO Cristiano Amon. This design aims to provide a starting point for robot manufacturers to develop AI-powered robots. The IQ10 is expected to accelerate the adoption of robots in various industries.
insight: Accelerating robot innovation with AI-powered robotics solutions globally

title: Humanoids are insanely hard to build, but Nvidia has a plan
summary: Nvidia and Unitree announced a humanoid robot reference design at GTC Taipei, highlighting advancements in robotics and artificial intelligence. The collaboration aims to address the challenges of building humanoid robots, which are notoriously difficult to create. This partnership may bring innovative solutions to the field.
insight: Nvidia's plan simplifies humanoid robot development with AI-driven reference designs

title: Intel intros OpenVINO for robots; 130 firms use Ultra Series 3
summary: Intel showcased Ella, a robot barista powered by the Core Ultra processor, at Computex. The upgrade to Series 3 simplified the system and increased return on investment.
insight: Intel simplifies AI-powered robotics with more efficient Ultra Series 3.

title: Intel tackles tracking of data center energy use with AET
summary: Intel has named Schwartz Digits an Application Energy Telemetry user. This move aims to improve the Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) for data centers. By leveraging AET, Intel seeks to better track and manage energy consumption.
insight: Efficient data center energy use can significantly reduce operational costs.

title: Concorde-style supersonic flights over US land could return, as long as they're quiet enough
summary: The US government proposes a rule to allow supersonic flights over land, as long as they meet noise standards.
insight: Quieter supersonic flights could make long-distance travel more environmentally friendly.

title: A leaked Microsoft experiment reveals a new OS built entirely around Copilot and AI agents
summary: Microsoft experimented with a cloud-based OS built around Copilot and AI agents, but its current status is unknown. The project's existence contradicts Microsoft's previous statements on Copilot's limited scope. This revelation suggests Microsoft may be more invested in AI-powered OS development than initially acknowledged.
insight: Microsoft's AI-powered OS ambitions may be more extensive than admitted.

title: Someone finally built a working Game Boy emulator for E-ink screens
summary: A YouTuber transformed the M5Stack's PaperS3 into a 60Hz E-ink Game Boy emulator, leveraging its paper-like display to achieve a surprisingly good match for the handheld's graphical capabilities.
insight: E-ink screens bring retro gaming to low-power, paper-like displays.

title: Meta is using old DDR4 memory in DDR5-only AI servers to save on hardware costs
summary: Meta is using old DDR4 memory in new 'MemServers' to cut costs, despite featuring AMD's powerful Epyc Turin CPUs.
insight: Cost savings compromise performance potential in Meta's AI servers.

title: A new attack uses a BioShock-style puzzle to convince AI browsers they're not in the real world
summary: Researchers from LayerX unveiled BioShocking, a vulnerability targeting AI-powered browsers. It bypasses security guardrails, allowing malicious instructions to be executed.
insight: AI browser security flaws can lead to autonomous task exploitation.

title: OpenAI may give the US government a 5% stake, following Trump's Intel playbook
summary: OpenAI may give the US government a 5% stake to distribute AI gains and improve industry-regulator relations.
insight: Sharing AI profits with the public improves industry-regulator relationships.

title: Dell just overtook HP as the top PC maker in the US as the entire market shrinks
summary: Dell surpassed HP as the top US PC maker in Q126, amidst declining market. PC shipments in the US fell 7% year-over-year in Q126, the steepest decline since Q323. The market decline is attributed to weak consumer demand.
insight: Market contraction forces companies to adapt and innovate for survival.

title: Google just lost its final appeal over Android, $4.7 billion EU fine stands
summary: Google loses final appeal over a $4.7 billion EU fine for abusing Android's dominant position, confirming the penalty.
insight: Google's business practices face significant regulatory scrutiny globally.

title: Rufus improves Windows installs, adds RISC-V and UEFI:NTFS fixes
summary: Rufus 4.15 improves Windows installs, adds RISC-V and UEFI:NTFS fixes, and resolves boot crashes.
insight: Enhanced bootability and reliability for various architectures and systems.

title: Intel raises prices on the Core Ultra 200S Plus CPUs people actually liked
summary: Intel has increased prices on its Core Ultra 7 270K Plus and Core Ultra 5 250K Plus CPUs.
insight: Increased prices may impact consumer purchasing decisions and demand.

title: Former Microsoft dev built a 2.5KB Notepad clone with zero AI features
summary: A former Microsoft developer created a 2.5KB Notepad clone called TinyRetroPad, mimicking Notepad's functionality.
insight: Minimalism in software development can lead to innovative simplicity solutions.

title: Apple expects its $2,500 foldable iPhone Ultra to sell 10 million units this year
summary: Apple reportedly plans to produce 10 million foldable iPhones this year.
insight: Foldable iPhone demand is exceeding initial supplier forecasts.

title: As Sony exits physical media, Microsoft is testing a way to link your Xbox discs to digital licenses
summary: Microsoft is testing linking Xbox discs to digital licenses, sparking speculation about its stance on physical games. This move has reignited concerns over whether Microsoft will follow Sony's decision to stop selling physical games. The development was recently leaked to The Verge.
insight: Shift to digital-only sales could significantly impact game industry business models

title: Former PlayStation boss calls Valve's Steam Machine "meh," asks if he's "going back to PS4 days"
summary: Shuhei Yoshida, former PlayStation boss, shares negative thoughts on Steam Machine.
insight: Steam Machines failed to impress even the gaming industry's own leaders.

title: A Microsoft Defender flaw is now being linked to ransomware attacks
summary: A Microsoft Defender flaw, BlueHammer, was leaked early by a researcher, reducing defenders' preparation time. The exploit details were released before Microsoft had a patch ready, potentially making it easier for attackers to exploit the vulnerability. This unusual move has been linked to ransomware attacks.
insight: Early exploitation of Microsoft flaw linked to increased ransomware attacks.
